message:
test(raptorq/gf256): multi-env override interaction tests and test helper refactoring

New test helpers for cleaner multi-override test setup:
- with_gf256_envs(): sets multiple env vars atomically within a clean
  GF256 environment, reducing boilerplate for interaction tests
- unsupported_profile_pack_env_value_for_kernel(): returns a profile
  pack name that is valid but unsupported on the current host
  architecture (e.g., x86-avx2 on ARM, aarch64-neon on x86)
- default_profile_metadata_for_kernel(): returns the canonical default
  profile metadata for the current kernel's architecture class

Two new interaction tests verify that multi-env override combinations
preserve fallback reasons while correctly scrubbing provenance:

1. unsupported_profile_request_with_forced_mode: requests an
   architecture-incompatible profile pack AND forces fused mode
   simultaneously. Verifies UnsupportedProfileForHost fallback is
   preserved, the profile falls back to the host default, the forced
   fused mode is applied, and all provenance metadata is scrubbed to
   manual-override constants.

2. unknown_profile_request_with_numeric_override: requests a
   completely unknown profile pack AND applies a numeric addmul
   override. Verifies UnknownRequestedProfile fallback is preserved,
   the numeric override is applied (addmul_min_total = 16384), the
   profile falls back to the host default, and provenance is scrubbed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
